newsletters.reason: Import AI is a top-tier AI newsletter. Topic: AI self-improvement / autonomous research agents. Directly relevant to LLM agent capabilities and AI safety. High signal for wiki enrichment.

newsletters.subject: 🎙️ This week on How I AI: The internal AI tool that's transforming how Stripe designs products
newsletters.reason: Stripe's internal AI tool for product design. Relevant to AI-in-enterprise, product development workflows, and practical AI tool adoption at scale. Good case study material.

newsletters.subject: The distillation panic
newsletters.reason: Model distillation is a core ML technique. 'Panic' suggests policy/regulatory/industry concern around knowledge distillation from large models. Directly relevant to LLM training, efficiency, and AI safety discourse.

newsletters.subject: $1.75 Billion Bet on Chinese AI
newsletters.reason: Major investment in Chinese AI sector. Relevant to AI ecosystem landscape, geopolitical AI competition, and funding trends. Good for tracking China AI ecosystem developments.

newsletters.subject: [AINews] The Other vs The Utility
newsletters.reason: swyx is a prominent AI developer/agent thinker. AINews is a curated AI links digest. 'The Other vs The Utility' suggests philosophical framing of AI differentiation vs commoditization. Relevant to AI strategy and developer tooling discourse.

newsletters.subject: 15 Privacy Questions Every AI Builder is Asking
newsletters.reason: AI privacy questions — tangentially relevant. Privacy is important but this is more about compliance/governance than core LLM/agent technology. Could be useful for an AI safety/governance wiki section if one exists.
